findings.message: Consumer pact not regenerated after response shape change: the function now expects 'reserved_count' field in the response (line 14) and returns a dict instead of int, but the consumer pact test (tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py:19) only defines 'in_stock' and expects an int return value (line 22). The committed pact doesn't include the new 'reserved_count' field, creating a contract mismatch.
findings.suggestion: Regenerate the consumer pact by running the consumer contract test (tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py) to update the pact with the new response shape and the new 'reserved_count' field. Update the consumer test assertion to match the new dict return type, then commit the regenerated pact file.

findings.message: Consumer test assertion broken by incompatible change: the test asserts `get_stock(...) == 5` (returns int), but the implementation (src/demo/clients/inventory.py:14) now returns a dict. This assertion will fail at runtime, indicating a weakened consumer contract.
findings.suggestion: Update the test assertion to match the new return type: `assert get_stock(str(srv.url), 1) == {'item_id': 1, 'in_stock': 5, 'reserved_count': <expected_value>}`. Also ensure the mock response includes the 'reserved_count' field.
